sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Milan are in solid form, remaining unbeaten in their last 9 matches with 3 wins and 2 draws in their last 5, showcasing a strong attacking output with goals in most games. Roma, on the other hand, have also performed well, securing 3 wins in their last 5 matches, including back-to-back victories, though they’ve struggled defensively in recent outings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Roma have had the upper hand with a 3-1 win in their most recent meeting, while Milan triumphed 3-1 in the earlier fixture at home. Historically, matches between these two often see both teams scoring, with 9 of the last 10 head-to-heads featuring goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Milan have scored 14 goals this season (4th in the league) and conceded only 7 (3rd best), showing balance, though they underperform their expected goals. Roma, with 10 goals scored and just 4 conceded, sit 3rd in the standings, reflecting a tighter defense and clinical finishing despite fewer big chances created.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: Milan’s Luka Modrić has been influential in midfield with a 7.64 average rating over the last 5 matches, while Roma’s Paulo Dybala stands out with an 8.12 rating against Milan historically. Trends suggest a high likelihood of over 2.5 goals and over 4.5 cards, given both teams’ recent stats and head-to-head history.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Roma’s slight edge in recent form and head-to-head dominance, combined with Sofascore’s 42% probability for an away win, they appear favorites. However, Milan’s unbeaten streak and home advantage at San Siro could make this a tight contest, likely resulting in a competitive draw or narrow Roma victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Milan holds a slender lead over Roma with a scoreline of 1-0 at the iconic San Siro. The home side capitalized on a late first-half goal by Strahinja Pavlović in the 39th minute to take the advantage. Despite Roma enjoying more possession and creating several opportunities, Milan made the most of their chances, showing clinical finishing in a tightly contested match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Milan's performance in the first half was marked by efficiency in front of goal, converting one of their key opportunities. Their defensive structure held firm despite Roma's higher share of the ball and frequent entries into the final third. Roma, on the other hand, dominated possession and showed intent with numerous attempts on goal, but they lacked the cutting edge to level the score. The away side also displayed strength in aerial duels and physical challenges, yet struggled to create clear-cut chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Milan, Strahinja Pavlović stood out not only for his goal but also for his defensive solidity, making crucial clearances. Rafael Leão was instrumental in the attack, providing the assist for the goal and posing a constant threat on the wing. On Roma's side, Matías Soulé and Wesley showed promise with their forward runs and attempts on target, while Bryan Cristante contributed with key passes in midfield. However, Roma will need more from Paulo Dybala if they are to turn things around in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Roma largely dictating the tempo early on with consistent attacking pressure and frequent spells of dominance in the Milan half. Their momentum was evident through prolonged periods of control, especially in the opening stages. However, Milan shifted the tide as the half progressed, particularly in the latter minutes, building up significant attacking phases that culminated in their goal. This late surge suggests Milan found their rhythm just before the break, while Roma's earlier momentum failed to yield results.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Roma are likely to push hard for an equalizer, leveraging their possession-based play and looking to exploit gaps in Milan's defense. Milan, buoyed by their goal and late first-half momentum, will aim to protect their lead while seeking opportunities on the counter. The match could open up if Roma commit more players forward, potentially leaving space for Milan's attacking players to exploit. Discipline will be key for both sides, as a single mistake could swing the result.
